title: There is nothing but a life of potential
body:
There is nothing but a life of potential
When you forget to struggle
When you surrender to rest
The pain then can soften

We cling to brittle systems
Finite as our grasp itself
The fatigued release, inevitable

In a foggy maze we cower 
Born of a wayward world
We seem to stretch and break
To find our way
Falling short
Spreading thin 

By will or surrender, we curl into ourselves
Drawn to inward pull
The pain can soften

Once brittle, now broken
Our parts fall into a familiar void
We witness and sway 
With unknowable breeze 
No longer resisting our journeys
Tumbling onto the scrap makers lap
To be built again by the winds of change
